insight: Founders building one-person AI company systems resist narrowing to one department because each agent is independently useful. The coordination layer (shared memory + event bus) is the real moat, but individual agent quality must come first per P1. Architecture: coordination layer ships first, then thin agents in parallel, then depth phase.

HITL for background/cron-triggered agents requires a notification channel (Slack/email) configured before background agents are enabled. Without it, the HITL invariant is unenforceable for scheduled tasks running at odd hours.

lesson: Build coordination layer first (shared memory + event bus + HITL) even for Phase 1. Agents built on top of coordination are coordination-aware from day one — retrofitting is 2x the work.
Any async HITL polling loop needs durable pending state written to memory BEFORE the first poll. Crash recovery is not optional for production HITL systems — the user never sees the dropped task.

In SCOPE EXPANSION mode, always separate BLOCKER TODOs from DEFERRED features in the plan. RC-1 and RC-3 (behavior tables + crash recovery) blocked the build even after 11 scope items were accepted — the plan looked complete but was not buildable without them.
